title: Upon Faunus.
content:
We read how Faunus, he the shepherds' god,
    His wife to death whipped with a myrtle rod.
    The rod, perhaps, was better'd by the name;
    But had it been of birch, the death's the same.
meaning:
"Upon Faunus." is a quintessential example of Robert Herrick's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
